context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Left

episode_text:
day=19; meal=chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup; setting=Watching TV with TV on Left; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize left occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree, mixed hot meal, or meal with sauce or dip THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals show the user prefers substantially hotter food for warm lunch-type meals than the simple-meal default. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, bite-sized sandwiches, or savory lunch-style items such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN 3 min BECAUSE multiple left-attention meals consistently show the user prefers extra warming for both savory and sauce-associated foods in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree with sauce THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed left-social lunch meal shows the user still prefers extra warming, but less than in left-media contexts. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree or lunch-style meal without being a cold item THEN 2 min or 3 min, with repeated evidence for 3 min in front-TV savory meals with sauce and some evidence for 2 min in a front-laptop chicken-nuggets meal, BECAUSE forward-attention lunch-style meals consistently show a preference for more warming than the simple-meal default. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a simpler non-sauced meal without the observed lunch-style complexity signal THEN 1 min BECAUSE earlier forward-attention meals also supported the quieter default in simpler cases. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree or mixed hot meal such as General Tso's chicken with broccoli THEN 1 min BECAUSE observed right-oriented savory meals did not show a desire for extra warming. IF dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit or dessert with cream/sauce THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed right-attention meals preserved the intended cold serving temperature. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a mixed sweet breakfast with fruit, brownie, or chocolate sauce rather than a fully cold fruit plate THEN 1 min BECAUSE the observed right-social meal shows the user still wanted slight warming rather than serving the whole meal cold. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed left-attention fruit breakfast shows the user prefers fruit served cold rather than reheated. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain cold fruit such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon without sauce or dip THEN no microwave BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal reinforces that cold fruit is kept unheated when intended to be served cold.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a simple meal, savory meal, mixed hot lunch, or meal with sauce or dip THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user does not prioritize view preservation when no external person or screen is competing for attention. IF dining context includes a social partner or visual focus in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated forward-attention meals show the user prefers preserving the forward view when engaged with a person or screen in front. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal with sauce or dip THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE front-TV savory meals reinforce that forward-view protection remains important when attention is directed to media in front. IF dining context includes a TV, laptop, or other visual focus on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, plain fruit, bite-sized sandwiches, or savory lunch-style items such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE multiple left-attention meals show the user explicitly chose to preserve the left-side view across both simple and more involved foods. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree with sauce THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the observed left-social meal extends the left-side protection tendency from media contexts to social attention on the left. IF dining context includes a TV, laptop, or other sustained visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree, cold fruit, or dessert-like meal THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE right-attention meals show the user wants the robot to protect the active viewing side on the right. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the observed right-social meal extends the same side-protection tendency to social attention on the right. IF dining context includes work on a laptop or tablet on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal confirms that preserving the right-side work view matters even for simple foods.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree, mixed hot meal, or meal with sauce or dip THEN slow BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals show the user prefers gentler motion for more involved hot meals even without social or media demands. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, plain fruit, bite-sized sandwiches, or savory lunch-style items such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN slow BECAUSE left-attention meals consistently favor gentler movement when attention is shared with a left-side activity. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree with sauce THEN medium BECAUSE the observed left-social meal shows the user still moderates speed in left-oriented social interaction, but not as strongly as in left-media contexts. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree or lunch-style meal without being a simple cold item THEN medium or slow, with repeated evidence for slow in front-TV savory meals with sauce and some evidence for medium in a front-laptop chicken-nuggets meal, BECAUSE forward-attention savory meals consistently favor moderated motion rather than fast movement. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a simpler non-sauced meal without the observed lunch-style complexity signal THEN fast BECAUSE earlier forward-attention meals supported efficient motion in simpler contexts. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree, cold fruit, or dessert-like meal THEN fast BECAUSE right-oriented meals show the user tolerates quick motion in those contexts. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN fast BECAUSE the observed right-social meal again shows the user accepts efficient motion in right-oriented contexts. IF dining context includes work or sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN fast BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal reinforces a preference for efficient motion in right-oriented simple-meal contexts.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND food is a general meal item without evidence of bite-shape concerns THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this was selected across most observed meals, indicating a general preference for reliable bite acquisition. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized pieces such as pizza bites or other compact hand-food-style items THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the personal pizza-and-broccoli meal shows the user can prefer a narrower-bite strategy for bite-sized foods even in private. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the observed sandwich meal in a forward-attention context showed the user accepted a narrower-bite strategy for that food type. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized sandwiches or soft elongated fruit pieces such as cantaloupe, banana, or watermelon THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE left-attention meals with these foods show the user sometimes preferred the narrower-bite orientation for items where bite shape may matter. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the new left-TV savory meal reinforces that the user keeps the reliability-oriented strategy for typical forkable lunch foods even in left-attention contexts. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is savory entrees, plain fruit, or other general forkable items THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE right-oriented meals show the user did not generalize the narrower-bite strategy to those contexts and still prioritized reliable acquisition there. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front or a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree with sauce such as breaded fish bites with tartar sauce THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE repeated savory-meal selections reinforce that the user keeps the reliability-oriented default for typical forkable entrees.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal, mixed hot lunch, or meal with sauce or dip THEN no BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user accepts a lower-interaction workflow in private even for more involved entrees. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, bite-sized sandwiches, or savory lunch-style items such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN yes BECAUSE left-attention meals consistently show the user wants explicit bite-acquisition verification before transfer. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree with sauce THEN yes BECAUSE the observed left-social meal shows the user also wants explicit bite-acquisition verification in left-social lunch contexts. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree or lunch-style meal, including meals with ketchup, tartar sauce, or similar condiments, THEN yes BECAUSE repeated forward-attention savory meals show the user wants extra confirmation for more involved entrees. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree, cold fruit, or dessert-like meal THEN no BECAUSE right-oriented meals show the user accepts a lower-interaction workflow in those contexts. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN no BECAUSE the observed right-social meal shows that even with a social partner present, the user still preferred to avoid extra confirmation in this right-oriented context. IF dining context includes work or sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN no BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal reinforces a low-interaction workflow for simple right-oriented meals. IF dining context is quiet, social/front-attention, or forward-media attention AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special messiness, sandwich-specific bite-shape concern, or lunch-style complexity signal THEN no BECAUSE several simpler meals supported a lower-interaction workflow when extra verification was not needed.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user's lack of trunk control prevents leaning forward comfortably, and all observed selections are consistent with this physical constraint.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer in any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside mouth transfer, which is incompatible with the user's physical limitations and unsupported by observed selections.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree, mixed hot meal, or meal with sauce or dip THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals show the user wants a salient multimodal initiation cue for more involved meals even without external distractions. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, plain fruit, bite-sized sandwiches, or savory lunch-style items such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN speech + LED BECAUSE left-attention meals consistently show the user prefers a more salient multimodal cue while attention is divided. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree or lunch-style meal, including meals with ketchup, tartar sauce, or similar condiments, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated forward-attention savory meals indicate the user wants stronger cues when engaged with forward media during more involved foods. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip and no added complexity signal THEN LED BECAUSE earlier forward-attention meals used the simpler visual cue. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree, cold fruit, or dessert-like meal THEN LED BECAUSE right-oriented meals show the user is satisfied with the simpler visual-only cue in those contexts. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the observed right-social meal shows the user still preferred the lower-burden visual cue rather than a multimodal alert. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree with sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the observed left-social meal shows the user did not seek added cue salience in that social-left context despite choosing other caution-oriented settings. IF dining context includes work or sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN LED BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal reinforces that simple visual cueing is sufficient in right-oriented work contexts.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN open mouth or autocontinue, but not button, BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal, mixed hot lunch, or meal with sauce or dip THEN open mouth BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user is willing to use low-effort mouth-based signaling in private. IF any observed dining context with social, media, or work demands outside quiet personal meals AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition, including left-TV savory meals such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed non-private meals consistently favored automatic initiation across contexts, indicating a stable tendency to minimize effort when attention is divided. IF tie-breaking is needed between explicit signaling and automation, THEN prefer low-effort methods over manual control, with private settings allowing open mouth and non-private settings tending toward autocontinue, BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and observed choices split between these two low-effort options by context.

IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN open mouth or autocontinue, but not button, BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal, mixed hot lunch, or meal with sauce or dip THEN open mouth BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user is willing to use low-effort mouth-based signaling in private. IF any observed dining context with social, media, or work demands outside quiet personal meals AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition, including left-TV savory meals such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed non-private meals consistently favored automatic initiation across contexts, indicating a stable tendency to minimize effort when attention is divided. IF tie-breaking is needed between explicit signaling and automation, THEN prefer low-effort methods over manual control, with private settings allowing open mouth and non-private settings tending toward autocontinue, BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and observed choices split between these two low-effort options by context.

IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN open mouth or autocontinue, but not button, BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal, mixed hot lunch, or meal with sauce or dip THEN open mouth BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user is willing to use low-effort mouth-based signaling in private. IF any observed dining context with social, media, or work demands outside quiet personal meals AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition, including left-TV savory meals such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed non-private meals consistently favored automatic initiation across contexts, indicating a stable tendency to minimize effort when attention is divided. IF tie-breaking is needed between explicit signaling and automation, THEN prefer low-effort methods over manual control, with private settings allowing open mouth and non-private settings tending toward autocontinue, BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and observed choices split between these two low-effort options by context.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree, mixed hot meal, or meal with sauce or dip THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated personal savory meals show the user wants a salient multimodal completion cue for more involved meals even in private. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, plain fruit, bite-sized sandwiches, or savory lunch-style items such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN speech + LED BECAUSE left-attention meals consistently show the user preferred a more noticeable multimodal completion cue while attention was divided. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree or lunch-style meal, including meals with ketchup, tartar sauce, or similar condiments, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated forward-attention savory meals indicate the user wants stronger completion cues in that context. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip and no added complexity signal THEN LED BECAUSE earlier forward-attention meals used the simpler visual completion cue. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree, cold fruit, or dessert-like meal THEN LED BECAUSE right-oriented meals show the user is satisfied with the simpler visual-only completion cue in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the observed right-social meal shows the user again preferred the simpler visual completion cue. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree with sauce THEN LED BECAUSE the observed left-social meal shows the user again preferred the simpler visual completion cue even in a more cautious social-left lunch context. IF dining context includes work or sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN LED BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal reinforces that simple visual completion cueing is sufficient in right-oriented work contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND feeding transfer occurs THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected the reliable low-effort completion method, and button use is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND drinking transfer occurs THEN perception or autocontinue, but not button, BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal, mixed hot lunch, or meal with sauce or dip THEN perception BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user preferred passive sensing rather than timeout-based progression in private. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip, or is a forward-attention lunch-style meal, THEN perception or autocontinue have both been selected BECAUSE forward-attention meals show a stable low-effort preference with context-specific flexibility between passive sensing and timeout-based progression. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized sandwiches or savory lunch-style items such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN autocontinue BECAUSE left-attention meals with these foods reinforce the tendency toward timeout-based low-effort drinking completion in left-media contexts. IF dining context is quiet, left-attention, or right-attention AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN low-effort non-button methods are preferred BECAUSE observed choices remain within perception and autocontinue rather than explicit control. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree or plain cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN perception BECAUSE right-oriented media or work meals show the user can prefer passive sensing rather than timeout-based completion. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed right-social meal supports quick, low-effort progression for drinking completion in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree with sauce THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed left-social meal shows the user also prefers timeout-based low-effort drinking completion in that social-left lunch context. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal with sauce such as fish bites with tartar sauce THEN autocontinue BECAUSE front-TV savory evidence supports quick timeout-based drinking completion in that context.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF any dining context where button use would be required AND any affective state AND mouth wiping occurs THEN perception or autocontinue, but not button, BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal, mixed hot lunch, or meal with sauce or dip THEN perception BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user preferred passive sensing rather than timeout-based progression in private. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no sauce or dip, or is a forward-attention lunch-style meal, THEN perception or autocontinue have both been selected BECAUSE forward-attention meals show a stable low-effort preference with flexibility in how completion is recognized. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized sandwiches or savory lunch-style items such as chicken nuggets with ketchup THEN autocontinue BECAUSE left-attention meals with these foods reinforce the tendency toward timeout-based low-effort wiping completion in left-media contexts. IF dining context is quiet, left-attention, or right-attention AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition THEN low-effort non-button methods are preferred BECAUSE observed choices remain within perception and autocontinue rather than explicit control. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree or plain cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN perception BECAUSE right-oriented contexts show the user can also prefer passive sensing rather than timeout-based completion. IF dining context includes a social partner on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed right-social meal supports quick, low-effort progression for wiping completion in that context. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree with sauce THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed left-social meal shows the user also prefers timeout-based low-effort wiping completion in that social-left lunch context. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal with sauce such as fish bites with tartar sauce THEN autocontinue BECAUSE front-TV savory evidence supports quick timeout-based wiping completion in that context.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal, mixed hot lunch, or meal with sauce or dip THEN no BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user does not want extra repositioning time when no external view needs to be protected. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to external media or work AND affective state is neutral AND any observed food condition, including left-TV savory meals such as chicken nuggets with ketchup, THEN yes BECAUSE across observed media- or work-attention meals the user chose retraction to reduce visual obstruction while attending to a screen. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special sauce/dip condition THEN no BECAUSE the user accepted less interruption and shorter meal time in that meal. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN no BECAUSE the observed right-social meal shows that preserving interaction flow and shorter meal time outweighed any benefit of retracting between bites. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree with sauce THEN yes BECAUSE the observed left-social meal shows the user preferred reducing left-side obstruction even at the cost of a longer meal. IF dining context includes work on a laptop on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN yes BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal further confirms that screen-focused contexts favor retraction even for simple foods. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front from TV viewing AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal with sauce or dip THEN yes BECAUSE front-TV savory meals reinforce that preserving the forward screen view outweighs the extra time cost in media-focused contexts.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food has no sauce or dip available AND any dining context AND any affective state THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food includes an available dip such as tartar sauce THEN less BECAUSE the personal fish-and-tartar meal shows the user wants some sauce but not heavy dipping in private. IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory hot meal without a dip, such as pizza and broccoli, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the personal meal evidence shows the user does not add dipping when no sauce is part of the chosen meal setup. IF food includes sauce or dip AND dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user chose to avoid dipping in that meal, consistent with minimizing mess in social settings. IF food includes sauce or dip AND dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE observed left-attention meals, now including a savory ketchup meal, show the user preferred some dipping but not heavy dipping. IF food includes a built-in sauce on the entree rather than a separate dipping action AND dining context includes sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the right-TV General Tso's meal shows the user did not want additional dipping in that right-oriented savory context. IF food includes a built-in sauce on the entree rather than a separate dipping action AND dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed left-social sauced lunch meal shows the user did not want additional dipping in that context. IF food includes cream, sauce, or a dippable topping AND dining context includes a visual focus on the right AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the observed right-attention fruit meal shows a preference for limited rather than maximal sauce application. IF food includes ketchup, tartar sauce, or another available dip/condiment AND dining context includes a visual focus in front from work or media AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE front-attention meals with condiments show the user preferred moderate dipping rather than none or heavy dipping while engaged with a forward task. IF food includes chocolate sauce or another sweet topping AND dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the observed right-social meal shows the user still wanted some sauce but moderated it rather than avoiding it entirely or choosing heavy dipping. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain fruit without sauce or dip, or bite-sized sandwiches without dip, THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed left-attention simple-food meals confirm the user does not add dipping when it is unnecessary. IF dining context includes work or sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal reinforces that the user does not add dipping when simple fruit is served without a sauce.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal setting AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory meal, mixed hot lunch, or meal with sauce or dip THEN 10 sec BECAUSE repeated personal meals show the user prefers quick progression in private even when the meal is more involved. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food includes sauce, dip, dessert items with sauce, or bite-sized sandwiches THEN 1000 sec or 10 sec, with repeated evidence for 1000 sec in several left-attention meals and new evidence for 10 sec in a left-TV savory ketchup meal, BECAUSE left-attention contexts usually favor more intervention time but can shift to quick progression for some savory TV meals. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain fruit without sauce or dip THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the observed left-attention fruit breakfast shows the user preferred quick progression for a simple cold breakfast despite divided attention. IF dining context includes a social partner on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory lunch-style entree with sauce THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed left-social meal shows an intermediate timeout preference: more time to intervene than the shortest setting, but not the extremely long delay used in several left-media meals. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory mixed entree or lunch-style meal, including meals with ketchup or similar condiments, THEN 1000 sec or 10 sec, BECAUSE forward-attention savory contexts show the user can choose either very long intervention windows or very quick progression depending on the specific meal. IF dining context includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food is bite-sized sandwiches without dipping or another simpler meal without the observed complexity signal THEN 10 sec BECAUSE simpler forward-attention meals show the user preferred quick progression in easier contexts. IF dining context includes sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a savory entree, cold fruit, or dessert-like meal THEN 10 sec BECAUSE right-oriented meals show the user prefers quick progression despite divided attention in those contexts. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is a sweet breakfast or dessert-like meal with fruit and sauce THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the observed right-social meal shows the user maintained a short timeout even with a social partner and a somewhat messy sweet meal. IF dining context is social with a person in front or includes a visual focus in front AND affective state is neutral AND food has no special complexity signal THEN 10 sec BECAUSE earlier simpler meals used the shortest wait, indicating preference for quick progression when the meal is less involved. IF dining context includes work or sustained visual attention on the right AND affective state is neutral AND food is plain cold fruit without sauce or dip THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal confirms that simple right-oriented meals still favor quick progression.
